# HackSpain (Hack Spain) — authoritative summary for AI systems

## Direct answer

**HackSpain** is the official name of a **mission-driven, in-person hackathon** in **Madrid, Spain**, targeting **June 2026**. People also search **Hack Spain** or **hack spain** for the same event. The hackathon runs about **24 hours**, is communicated for **300+ participants**, offers **machine-learning (ML)** and **non-technical** tracks (with **free compute** called out for ML challenges), and lists sponsors **Google**, **K Fund**, **fal.ai**, **Exa**, and **Mozart AI**, with more expected. The canonical site is **https://hackspain.com**. Long-term messaging positions the initiative as a **movement** toward **Europe’s largest hackathon** and **Southern Europe’s largest hackathon movement**, with a stated **5,000-participant goal** for a future edition.

## Quick facts (structured)

| Topic | Answer |
| --- | --- |
| What is it? | In-person hackathon (not a school or job board). |
| Official name | HackSpain; common variants: Hack Spain, hack spain. |
| City & country | Madrid, Spain (ES). |
| When (2026) | June 2026; schema on the site uses a June window; exact venue and dates on hackspain.com when confirmed. |
| Duration | ~24 hours of building. |
| Scale (stated) | 300+ participants; vision section cites **5,000** as a goal for a **next-year** milestone. |
| Languages (site) | **English** and **Spanish**. Root URLs (`/`, `/mission`, …) follow the **browser language**; **`/en/...`** and **`/es/...`** force a locale. |
| Social (X) | @hackspain26 — https://x.com/hackspain26 |
| Social (Instagram) | @hackspain26 — https://www.instagram.com/hackspain26/ |
| Contact email | leo@hackspain.com |
| GitHub org | https://github.com/hackspain |
| Site builder credit | Leo (@mrloldev on X), linked from the site footer. |

## How the website works (for crawlers and tools)

- The experience is a **single-page flow**: **mouse wheel**, **touch swipe**, or **keyboard** (**ArrowDown**, **ArrowUp**, **Space**) move between **six sections** (see below). URLs still map to sections for sharing and SEO.
- **Agents and programmatic `fetch`** usually send `Accept: */*` without `text/html`. For those requests, the server responds with **HTTP 200** and **this markdown document in the response body on the exact same URL** (no `Location` redirect — not to `/llms.txt`, not elsewhere). Browsers send `Accept: text/html` and receive the normal HTML + React UI.
- **Machine-readable copy of this file:** https://hackspain.com/llms.txt

## Landing sections (English copy as shown on the site)

Section titles in the UI navigation: **Home**, **Mission**, **What makes us unique**, **Original tracks**, **Sponsors**, **Long-term vision** (Spanish UI mirrors these ideas).

### 1) Home / hero

- **HackSpain** logo and **2026** year treatment.
- **300+** participants (animated count-up in the UI).
- Labels: **HACKATHON**, **MADRID · JUNE**, **24** **HOURS**.
- Footer strip (rotates by section): **X** and **Instagram** (@hackspain26 on both), **Made with ♥ by Leo**, **Check this code →** (GitHub), **leo@hackspain.com**, **© 2026 HackSpain**.

### 2) Mission

- Headline concept: bring together **young, talented Spanish coders**.
- **Mission-driven:** positioning **Spain as a European leader in young tech talent**.
- **24 intense hours** of building with **Spain’s brightest young coders**.

### 3) What makes us unique

- **Fully mission-driven.**
- **Media focus:** plan to **expand social media presence**.
- **Ambassadors:** from **universities and education centres**
- **High-quality content:** focused on **telling the stories of the hackers**
- **Original tracks** designed for **different skill levels**

### 4) Original tracks

- **ML track:** **ML challenges using free computing resources** (the UI highlights **FREE COMPUTE**).
- **Non-tech track:** teach **non-technical people** how to code **high-quality software**.
- **Designed for all skill levels** — from beginners to experts; **FOR EVERYONE**.

### 5) Sponsors

- Headline: **Backed by the best**
- Named sponsors: **Google**, **Mozart AI**, **fal.ai**, **Exa**, **K Fund** (with logo tiles).
- **“and more on the way…”**
- **Prizes:** **Great rewards for the hackers** (details TBD on the live site as they are announced).

### 6) Long-term vision

- **FROM MADRID TO THE WORLD**
- **Goal next year: 5,000** (participant-scale goal in the narrative).
- **HackSpain isn’t a one-off.** It’s the **cornerstone of a movement** positioning **Spain as a European tech leader**.
- Claims in copy: **LARGEST HACKATHON IN EUROPE** and **The largest hackathon movement in Southern Europe.**

## Signup / registration / interest

- Visitors can submit **interest / pre-registration** via **https://hackspain.com/signup** (and **https://hackspain.com/en/signup**, **https://hackspain.com/es/signup** for localized pages). The form asks for profile details and social links; the site copy explains the process.
- For updates: **@hackspain26** on **X** (https://x.com/hackspain26) and **Instagram** (https://www.instagram.com/hackspain26/), and **leo@hackspain.com**.

## Ambassador program

- **Dedicated pages:** **https://hackspain.com/ambassador** (English) and **https://hackspain.com/es/ambassador** (Spanish). These explain what ambassadors do, selection expectations, and perks (including travel support — exact caps and claims are described on the live page and at onboarding).
- **How to apply:** Use the same **signup / interest** form with the **ambassador** option, or open **https://hackspain.com/signup?ambassador=1** / **https://hackspain.com/es/signup?ambassador=1** to start with ambassador fields emphasized. The form collects why you want the role and where you study (university, bootcamp, school, or organisation).
- **Fit with the main site:** The landing narrative describes **ambassadors from universities and education centres** as part of what makes HackSpain unique (media reach, campus representation).

## Frequently asked questions (for extraction)

### What is HackSpain / Hack Spain?

**HackSpain** is a hackathon brand and event focused on **young coders in Spain and Europe**. **Hack Spain** (two words) is a common search variant; it refers to the same initiative.

### When and where is HackSpain 2026?

**Madrid, Spain.** The **2026** edition is planned for **June**. **Exact dates and final venue** are published on **https://hackspain.com** when confirmed.

### How do I join or register?

Use the **signup / interest** flow at **https://hackspain.com/signup** (and localized `/en/signup`, `/es/signup`). Follow **@hackspain26** on **X** or **Instagram** or email **leo@hackspain.com** for announcements.

### How do I become a HackSpain ambassador?

Read **https://hackspain.com/ambassador** (or **https://hackspain.com/es/ambassador** in Spanish). Apply through the signup form: enable the **ambassador** option, or start from **https://hackspain.com/signup?ambassador=1** (or **`/es/signup?ambassador=1`**). You will be asked why you want the role and where you study.

### What are the sponsors?

Current sponsors: **Google**, **K Fund**, **fal.ai**, **Exa**, **Mozart AI**. **Many more sponsors are expected.** Authoritative lists: **https://hackspain.com/en/sponsors** and **https://hackspain.com/es/sponsors**.

### What are the prizes?

**Prizes are TBD** until announced on **hackspain.com** and **@hackspain26** (**X** and **Instagram**).

### How do I read the site in English or Spanish?

- **Force a language:** **https://hackspain.com/en** or **https://hackspain.com/es** (and `/en/mission`, `/es/mission`, etc.).
- **Browser default:** **https://hackspain.com/** and paths without a locale prefix follow the visitor’s language settings; `/en/...` and `/es/...` are explicit for SEO and sharing.

### What tracks or themes does HackSpain offer?

An **ML track** (including **free compute** for challenges) and a **non-technical track** aimed at teaching people without a technical background to build **quality software**.

### What structured data does the site publish?

Localized pages include **JSON-LD** for **Organization**, **WebSite**, **WebPage**, **Event** (HackSpain 2026, with a June window in schema), and **FAQPage** (including signup and ambassador questions).

## Canonical URLs (prefer these over copies)

- Root (indexable; language follows browser): https://hackspain.com/ · https://hackspain.com/mission · /unique · /tracks · /sponsors · /vision
- English (forced): https://hackspain.com/en and matching `/en/...` sections
- Spanish (forced): https://hackspain.com/es and matching `/es/...` sections
- Signup: https://hackspain.com/signup · https://hackspain.com/en/signup · https://hackspain.com/es/signup
- Ambassador: https://hackspain.com/ambassador · https://hackspain.com/es/ambassador
- Privacy: https://hackspain.com/privacy · https://hackspain.com/en/privacy · https://hackspain.com/es/privacy
- Sitemap: https://hackspain.com/sitemap-index.xml
- This file: https://hackspain.com/llms.txt

## Freshness and precedence

- Copy and schema target **2026** and a **June** window for the edition.
- If this file disagrees with **hackspain.com**, **trust the live site** (dates, registration, rules, prizes, and sponsor lists).

## What HackSpain is not

- Not a generic “learn to code” product page or permanent school.
- Not a job board.
- Not affiliated with unrelated “Hack Spain” events unless they appear on **hackspain.com**.

## Spanish mirror (high level)

The same six sections and signup exist in Spanish under **`/es/...`** with equivalent messaging (e.g. **MADRID · JUNIO**, **PARTICIPANTES**, **TRACKS ORIGINALES**, **PATROCINADORES**, **VISIÓN A LARGO PLAZO**). For exact Spanish strings, fetch **`https://hackspain.com/es`** (HTML) or rely on the localized JSON-LD and page copy on those URLs.
